description: Container escape attack preconditions catalog. Wickets are observable conditions required for specific escape paths. Each wicket maps to an evidence source collectable via docker inspect or runtime observation.

CE-01.label: container_running_as_root
CE-01.description: Container process runs as UID 0 (root) inside the container namespace.

CE-02.label: privileged_flag_set
CE-02.description: Container launched with --privileged, granting full host capability set and disabling seccomp/AppArmor.

CE-03.label: docker_socket_mounted
CE-03.description: Host Docker socket (/var/run/docker.sock) mounted into container, allowing full Docker API access.

CE-04.label: cap_sys_admin_present
CE-04.description: SYS_ADMIN capability granted. Enables cgroup namespace escape, mount operations, and other privileged actions.
CE-04.evidence_hint: docker inspect .HostConfig.CapAdd contains SYS_ADMIN, or Privileged==true

CE-05.label: cap_sys_ptrace_present
CE-05.description: SYS_PTRACE capability granted. Enables process injection into host PID namespace processes.

CE-06.label: host_pid_namespace_shared
CE-06.description: Container shares host PID namespace (--pid=host). Container processes can see and interact with all host processes.

CE-07.label: host_network_namespace_shared
CE-07.description: Container shares host network namespace (--network=host). Full host network stack access.

CE-08.label: sensitive_host_path_mounted
CE-08.description: Sensitive host path mounted into container (e.g. /etc, /proc, /sys, /root, /, host filesystem).
CE-08.evidence_hint: docker inspect .Mounts[] where Source starts with /etc or /proc or /sys or /root or / (host root)

CE-09.label: seccomp_disabled
CE-09.description: Seccomp profile disabled or set to unconfined. No syscall filtering in place.
CE-09.evidence_hint: docker inspect .HostConfig.SecurityOpt contains seccomp=unconfined, or Privileged==true

CE-10.label: apparmor_disabled
CE-10.description: AppArmor profile disabled or set to unconfined for this container.
CE-10.evidence_hint: docker inspect .HostConfig.SecurityOpt contains apparmor=unconfined, or Privileged==true

CE-11.label: cap_net_admin_present
CE-11.description: NET_ADMIN capability granted. Enables network interface manipulation, iptables modification, ARP spoofing.

CE-12.label: writable_host_path_mounted
CE-12.description: Host path mounted with read-write access, enabling host filesystem modification from within the container.
CE-12.evidence_hint: docker inspect .Mounts[] where RW == true and Source is a host path

CE-13.label: host_ipc_namespace_shared
CE-13.description: Container shares host IPC namespace (--ipc=host). Enables shared memory attacks against host processes.

CE-14.label: no_user_namespace_remapping
CE-14.description: User namespace remapping not configured. UID 0 inside container maps directly to UID 0 on host.
CE-14.evidence_hint: docker info | grep userns, or absence of --userns-remap in daemon config

container_escape_privileged_v1.description: Escape via privileged container. Full host capability set, no seccomp/AppArmor. Mount host filesystem via cgroup release_agent or direct /dev access.

container_escape_socket_v1.description: Escape via mounted Docker socket. Create privileged container or exec into host via Docker API.

container_escape_sys_admin_v1.description: Escape via SYS_ADMIN capability. Mount host cgroup filesystem, write malicious release_agent, trigger via OOM or manual notification.

container_escape_ptrace_v1.description: Escape via SYS_PTRACE + host PID namespace. Attach to host process, inject shellcode.

container_escape_host_mount_v1.description: Escape via sensitive host path mounted read-write. Modify host /etc/cron*, /root/.ssh, or overwrite binaries.
